summary: First-finish smoke test for RCB Events CRM. Backend auth, dashboard stats, packages, bookings, payments, expenses endpoints all work with JWT. Frontend login → dashboard → bookings CRUD → calendar → packages → pipeline (lead creation, 7 kanban columns) → payments → expenses → vendors → staff → logout → protected route redirect all pass.
passed: POST /api/auth/login returns JWT token for admin@rcbevents.com
GET /api/auth/me returns admin profile with Bearer token
GET /api/packages returns 4 seeded packages (Standard, Gold, Gold Plus, Diamond)
GET /api/dashboard/stats returns all stat fields (net_revenue computed on FE)
POST /api/bookings creates booking with auto booking_number 'RCB-YYMMDD-XXXX' prefix
POST /api/payments records payment tied to booking_id
Login UI redirects to Dashboard, all 8 stat cards render (Total Bookings, Today, Upcoming, Completed, Revenue, Pending Payments, Total Expenses, Net Revenue) with Recent Bookings section
Booking creation via UI - new row appears with RCB- prefix visible
Calendar, Packages, Payments, Expenses, Vendors, Staff pages load with respective data-testid page containers
Pipeline page shows 7 kanban columns; lead creation via new-lead-btn works and appears in Lead column
Logout clears session and redirects to /login
Protected route /bookings redirects to /login when unauthenticated
notes: Booking API returns field name 'booking_number' (not 'booking_no'). Value starts with 'RCB-' as required.
Backend /api/dashboard/stats returns 7 fields; 'net_revenue' is computed client-side from revenue - total_expenses (verified visible on dashboard as '₹-500').
Leads endpoint requires 'mobile' field (frontend already sends this correctly).
